README (segment 2)
evidence.excerpt: ![Platform](https://img.shields.io/badge/platform-Linux%20%7C%20macOS-blue.svg) ![Go Version](https://img.shields.io/badge/go-1.25+-00ADD8.svg) ![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT%2BOpenAI%2FAnthropic%20Rider-blue.svg) ![Release](https://img.shields.io/github/v/release/Dicklesworthstone/ntm?include_prereleases)

evidence.title: README (segment 3)
evidence.excerpt: NTM turns `tmux` into a local control plane for multi-agent software development. It combines session orchestration, graph-aware work triage, safety policy and approvals, Agent Mail coordination, durable state capture, machine-readable robot surfaces, and a local REST/WebSocket API in one Go binary.

evidence.title: README (segment 4)
evidence.excerpt: ```bash curl -fsSL "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/main/install.sh?$(date +%s)" | bash -s -- --easy-mode ```

evidence.title: README (segment 5)
evidence.excerpt: Running several coding agents in parallel is easy to start and annoying to sustain. Plain `tmux` gives you panes, but it does not give you durable coordination, work selection, safety policy, approvals, history, replayable automation surfaces, or a shared control model that both humans and agents can use.

evidence.title: README (segment 6)
evidence.excerpt: - spawning labeled multi-agent sessions in `tmux` - sending work, interrupts, and follow-ups across panes - triaging what to do next with `br` and `bv` - coordinating agents with Agent Mail, file reservations, and assignments - protecting dangerous operations with policy, approvals, and guards - exposing the whole system through `--robot-*`, REST, SSE, WebSocket, and OpenAPI - capturing state with checkpoints, timelines, audit trails, and pipeline state

evidence.title: README (segment 7)
evidence.excerpt: | Area | What NTM provides | Typical commands | | --- | --- | --- | | Session orchestration | Spawn, label, inspect, zoom, dashboard, palette | `ntm spawn`, `ntm dashboard`, `ntm palette` | | Work intelligence | Graph-aware triage, next-step selection, impact analysis, assignment | `ntm work triage`, `ntm work next`, `ntm assign` | | Coordination | Human overseer mail, inbox views, file reservations, worktrees | `ntm mail`, `ntm locks`, `ntm worktrees` | | Safety | Destructive-command protection, policy editing, approval workflows | `ntm safety`, `ntm policy`, `ntm approve`, `ntm guards` | | Durable operations | Checkpoints, timelines, audit logs, saved sessions, pipelines | `ntm checkpoint`, `ntm timeline`, `ntm audit`, `ntm pipeline` | | Automation surfaces | Robot JSON, REST API, SSE/WebSocket streams, OpenAPI | `ntm --robot-snapshot`, `ntm serve`, `ntm openapi generate` |

evidence.title: README (segment 8)
evidence.excerpt: NTM is a pure Go project, but the runtime experience is intentionally integration-heavy.

evidence.title: v1.10.0
evidence.excerpt: ## NTM v1.10.0 **Named Tmux Manager** - Orchestrate AI coding agents in tmux sessions with beautiful TUI interfaces. ### Quick Install ```bash # One-liner install (Linux/macOS) curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/main/install.sh | bash # Homebrew (macOS/Linux) brew install dicklesworthstone/tap/ntm # Go install go install github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/cmd/ntm@v1.10.0 ``` ### Shell Integration Add to your shell rc file: ```bash eval "$(ntm shell zsh)" # for zsh eval "$(ntm shell bash)" # for bash eval "$(ntm shell fish)" # for fish ``` ## Changelog ### Features * 270835ea0db5bef5f9d7cfb5df83dad15ddf8be2: feat(config): unified retry policy and routing config sections (@Dicklesworthstone) * 00f2b6e6cd2bb22be713e603ed044bf29c407d18: feat(config): wire Print, GetValue, Diff, and Validate for remaining config sections (@Dicklesworthstone) * 0accb65a09dadc04c30a491f546ed03ba9d1ae33: feat(config): wire new sections and consolidate project path resolution (@Dicklesworthstone) * 97e61283529faff693a5dc1510e65a144fb3d0e5: feat(models): canonical model registry as single source of truth (@Dicklesworthstone) * 4653c9901c09af062cfdda335228e73b036a1a18: feat(robo…
